target.title: Prevent repeated byte-triggered compactions
target.body:
## Summary

- prevent repeated byte-triggered preflight compactions for the same active transcript when a previous byte-triggered compaction did not materially reduce the transcript below the configured threshold
- record byte-triggered compaction retry state on `SessionEntry` so retries require the same threshold, the same active session file, and material new growth
- preserve token-triggered preflight compaction behavior, including token compaction taking precedence when the byte retry is suppressed

## What Problem This Solves

When `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes` is configured, a byte-triggered preflight compaction can legitimately leave the active successor transcript above the threshold (for example when transcript rotation preserves branch/state history). Without retry hysteresis, the next turn can immediately trigger another byte-based preflight compaction, producing a repeated compaction loop.

This patch records the post-compaction active transcript size/file/threshold for byte-triggered compactions and suppresses another byte-triggered retry for that same active transcript until it grows materially. A retry is allowed when the active file changes, the threshold changes, or the transcript grows by at least `max(1 MiB, 10%)` from the previously recorded byte size.

Token-triggered compaction remains unchanged.

## Related

Adjacent to #88188 because both involve `maxActiveTranscriptBytes` behavior, but this PR handles the opposite failure mode: byte-triggered preflight compaction firing repeatedly after a prior byte-triggered compaction leaves the active transcript above threshold.

## Real setup tested

Source-level regression validation in a clean upstream checkout on the current branch. No production runtime or private deployment state was used for PR validation.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

From the repository checkout:

```bash
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-updates.ts \
  src/config/sessions/types.ts \
  src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-updates.test.ts

git diff --check

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts -- --run

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-updates.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-updates.lifecycle.test.ts -- --run

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs \
  -p tsconfig.core.json \
  --incremental \
  --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-byteguard.tsbuildinfo
```

## Evidence

- `agent-runner-memory.test.ts`: 53 tests passed
- `session-updates.test.ts` + `session-updates.lifecycle.test.ts`: 5 tests passed
- `tsgo` core project check passed
- formatting check passed
- diff whitespace check passed

New coverage includes:

- first byte-threshold crossing still triggers preflight compaction
- same active transcript + same threshold + no material growth suppresses repeated byte-triggered compaction
- changed active session file does not suppress retry
- changed threshold does not suppress retry
- material transcript growth allows retry
- token-triggered compaction still runs when byte retry is suppressed
- rotated successor file and byte size are recorded after byte-triggered compaction
- byte-triggered metadata is persisted through `incrementCompactionCount`
- ordinary token compactions do not write byte-triggered retry metadata

## Observed result after fix

The repeated byte-trigger retry condition is blocked for the same active transcript until there is material new growth, while token-triggered compaction and legitimate byte retries remain available.

## What was not tested

- No production runtime validation was performed for this PR.
- This PR does not change successor transcript pruning semantics; it only prevents immediate retry loops when a byte-triggered compaction does not materially shrink the active transcript.
- End-to-end UI notification behavior is not changed or tested here.
target.author: anyech
